User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Alisraa International website, primarily for authentication and authorization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user (matches Firebase Auth UID).

isAdmin.description: Indicates whether the user has administrative privileges. Note: Actual admin status is controlled by the `roles_admin` collection.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents a single message within the real-time chat feature.

id.description: Unique identifier for the chat message.

senderId.description: The UID of the user who sent the message.

displayName.description: The display name of the sender (e.g., 'Guest' or username).

message.description: The content of the chat message.

timestamp.description: The server-side timestamp indicating when the message was sent.

definition.description: Stores public user profile data. Each user can manage their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: A global collection storing all chat messages for the live chat feature. Authenticated users (including anonymous) can read and write.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the chat message.

definition.description: A document's existence in this collection indicates that the user with the matching UID is an administrator. This enables fast, existence-based role checks in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is an admin.

firestore.reasoning: This simplified Firestore structure supports authentication, a global real-time chat, and role-based access for an admin dashboard. Path-based ownership is used for user profiles, while a global collection is used for chat to simplify implementation. Admin roles are managed by checking for the existence of a user's UID in the `roles_admin` collection, which is a performant and secure pattern for authorization.
